content.problemStatement:
You are given a rooted tree with NNN nodes (numbered 111 through NNN); the root is node 111. For each valid iii, node iii has weight wiw_iwi​, which is either 000 or 111.

We want to traverse the tree using depth first search. The order in which the nodes are visited is not uniquely defined, since we may visit the children of each node in an arbitrary order. Formally, the pseudocode of DFS-traversal is:

`function DFS(node n):
	node n is visited
	for each node s (s is a son of n) in some order:
		call DFS(s)
	return
call DFS(root)
`For each possible DFS-traversal of the tree, consider the sequence of weights of nodes in the order in which they are visited; each node is visited exactly once, so this sequence has length NNN. Calculate the number of inversions for each such sequence. The minimum of these numbers is the treeversion of our tree.

Find the treeversion of the given tree.
constraints: the graph on the input is a tree
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10,00010,00010,000
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers w1,w2,…,wNw_1, w_2, \ldots, w_Nw1​,w2​,…,wN​.
Each of the following N−1N-1N−1 lines contains two space-separated integers xxx and yyy denoting that nodes xxx and yyy are connected by an edge.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer — the treeversion of the given tree.
